title: base_url::new(): dead is_empty() branch in path check
description: client.rs:38: 'if path \!= "/" && \!path.is_empty()' — for http/https schemes, url::Url::parse always sets path to at least '/', so path.is_empty() is unreachable. The dead branch implies two distinct failure modes exist when only one does; a future contributor simplifying this check could unintentionally weaken it. The correct check is simply 'if path \!= "/"'. Note: this is distinct from 5y1i.6 (double-strip in fetch_session) and 5y1i.21 (query/fragment not rejected) — this is a dead code path in the construction-time validator itself.
